§6Uranium§f

§aThe most common radioactive element — the foundation of nuclear technology!§f

§bUranium§f is the most readily accessible radioactive element in NTM. Most early nuclear science revolves around refining and breeding it.

§4 Forms§f:
§b• Natural Uranium§f: unenriched, mildly radioactive, used in various processes
§b• U-238§f: non-fissile depleted uranium, byproduct of enrichment — used in weapons and armor due to high density
§b• U-235§f: fissile uranium, highly reactive — used in fuels and nuclear weapons
§b• Uranium Fuel§f: a mixture of U-238 and U-235 at §e5:1§f ratio

§6Production§f:
§b• Mining§f: found deep underground (y=5 to 25) in small pockets. §e7§f veins per chunk, §e5§f ore per vein.
§b• Ore processing§f: standard ore processing chain
§b• Radon gas§f: mining releases radon (radioactive!) — use respirators

§6Enrichment§f:
§b• Gas Centrifuge§f: early-game method to separate U-235 from U-238
§b• SILEX§f: late-game method using visible light — full isotopic separation with less infrastructure

§eTip§f: Centrifuging uranium ore yields small amounts of §bRadium-226§f, useful as a neutron source. Always use Gas Centrifuges for the early enrichment cycle!
